First, I'll go through the passage again to make sure I understand the key points. The passage talks about Mary Santiago, a shy girl who loves to dance and wants to get into a top dance school. She meets Joey Parker, a famous pop singer, at a ball. They fall in love, but when Mary leaves, she forgets her MP3 player, which Joey uses to find her. There's a wicked stepmother named Dominique Blatt who treats Mary like a maid and tries to stop her from succeeding. The story is a modern version of Cinderella, with some twists. The film focuses on following your dreams rather than looks or materialism. Now, looking at the options: A) Joey is just like other boys in Mary's class. But the passage says Joey is "everything the rest of the boys in her class are not—kind, handsome and desirable." So A is the opposite of what's stated. So A is incorrect. B) Mary's mother influences her a lot in singing. The passage mentions that Mary's mother died and Dominique took her in. It says Mary loves to dance, but there's no mention of her mother influencing her in singing. In fact, the mother's death led to Dominique taking her in. So B might not be correct. The story is about dancing, not singing. Also, Joey is the singer, not Mary. So B is probably wrong. C) Not many people have a dream to be realized. The passage starts by saying "there is a hidden Cinderella in everyone's heart—we all wish we could achieve recognition or success after a period of obscurity." So the author is saying that everyone has this dream, which contradicts option C. So C is incorrect. D) The MP3 player helps Joey in finding Mary.
